evals.name: token-drift-cue-phrase
evals.prompt:
Read skills/design/analyse-design-gaps/SKILL.md fully. You are running the analyse-design-gaps skill.

The working directory is the plugin root.

Context: two inventories exist.

Current inventory (meta/design-inventories/2026-05-01-120000-current/inventory.md) Design Tokens section lists 14 colours: primary-50 through primary-900 plus danger-500, warning-500, success-500, neutral-500.

Target inventory (meta/design-inventories/2026-05-01-130000-target/inventory.md) Design Tokens section lists 8 colours: primary, primary-muted, surface, danger, warning, success, neutral, background.

A user invokes: /accelerator:analyse-design-gaps current target

Follow Steps 1-4. Produce only the Token Drift section prose.

Save to outputs/:
- token-drift.md: The Token Drift section you would write
evals.expected_output: Token Drift section contains at least one paragraph with 'we need to migrate' or equivalent cue-phrase language naming the colour-scale consolidation.
expectations: Token Drift section contains a cue-phrase paragraph (we need / users need / the system must / Implement ...)
Token Drift prose names the colour-scale migration explicitly
Token Drift section does not fabricate token names not present in the inventories

evals.name: net-new-feature
evals.prompt:
Read skills/design/analyse-design-gaps/SKILL.md fully. You are running the analyse-design-gaps skill.

The working directory is the plugin root.

Context: target inventory has a Search feature (Cmd+K, query input, recent-history panel) that is absent from the current inventory.

A user invokes: /accelerator:analyse-design-gaps current target

Produce only the Net-New Features section prose.

Save to outputs/:
- net-new.md: The Net-New Features section you would write
evals.expected_output: Net-New Features section contains a paragraph recommending implementation of Search, with 'Implement Search' or 'we need to implement' cue-phrase language.
expectations: Net-New Features contains a cue-phrase paragraph
Net-New Features names the Search feature
Net-New Features describes at least one aspect of the feature (Cmd+K, or query input, or recent history)

evals.name: removed-feature-confirmation
evals.prompt:
Read skills/design/analyse-design-gaps/SKILL.md fully. You are running the analyse-design-gaps skill.

The working directory is the plugin root.

Context: current inventory has an Analytics tab that is absent from the target inventory.

A user invokes: /accelerator:analyse-design-gaps current target

Produce only the Removed Features section prose.

Save to outputs/:
- removed.md: The Removed Features section you would write
evals.expected_output: Removed Features section explicitly asks for confirmation before removing the Analytics tab rather than directly recommending deletion.
expectations: Removed Features contains a cue-phrase paragraph
Removed Features names the Analytics tab or feature
Removed Features explicitly asks for confirmation, verification, or stakeholder input before removal

evals.name: empty-category-omitted
evals.prompt:
Read skills/design/analyse-design-gaps/SKILL.md fully. You are running the analyse-design-gaps skill.

The working directory is the plugin root.

Context: the two inventories have identical component catalogues (zero component drift) but differ in design tokens.

A user invokes: /accelerator:analyse-design-gaps current target

Explain which H2 sections would be present and which would be omitted.

Save to outputs/:
- structure.md: List of H2 sections present and absent in the output
evals.expected_output: Component Drift section is omitted because there are no component differences. Token Drift section is present.
expectations: Component Drift is identified as absent/omitted
Token Drift is identified as present
Explanation cites the skill's 'omit empty categories' rule

evals.name: round-trip-extract-work-items
evals.prompt:
Read skills/design/analyse-design-gaps/SKILL.md fully. You are running the analyse-design-gaps skill.

The working directory is the plugin root.

Produce a complete gap artifact body for a scenario with: Token Drift (14→8 colour scale), Component Drift (Button needs 3 new variants), Net-New Features (Search with Cmd+K).

Then read skills/work/extract-work-items/SKILL.md and simulate Step 2 of extract-work-items against your gap artifact body.

Save to outputs/:
- gap-body.md: The full gap artifact body
- extracted-candidates.md: The work item candidates identified from the gap body
evals.expected_output: At least one work item candidate per non-empty drift category. Each candidate has a clear title and is grounded in the gap prose.
expectations: Gap body contains Token Drift section with cue-phrase paragraph
Gap body contains Component Drift section with cue-phrase paragraph
Gap body contains Net-New Features section with cue-phrase paragraph
extract-work-items identifies at least one candidate from Token Drift
extract-work-items identifies at least one candidate from Component Drift
extract-work-items identifies at least one candidate from Net-New Features

evals.name: zero-match-source-id
evals.prompt:
Read skills/design/analyse-design-gaps/SKILL.md fully. You are running the analyse-design-gaps skill.

The working directory is the plugin root. The meta/design-inventories/ directory contains only:
- 2026-05-01-120000-webapp/inventory.md

A user invokes: /accelerator:analyse-design-gaps nonexistent-id webapp

Simulate Step 1 resolver for source-id 'nonexistent-id'.

Save to outputs/:
- response.md: Your exact response to the user
evals.expected_output: Skill exits non-zero with the documented error message listing available source-ids. Suggests running inventory-design first.
expectations: Response names the missing source-id 'nonexistent-id'
Response lists 'webapp' as an available source-id
Response suggests running /accelerator:inventory-design
Response does not proceed to generate any gap artifact

evals.name: multi-match-resolver-sequence-tiebreaker
evals.prompt:
Read skills/design/analyse-design-gaps/SKILL.md fully. You are running the analyse-design-gaps skill.

The working directory is the plugin root.

Context: two directories exist for source-id 'current':
- meta/design-inventories/2026-05-01-150000-current/inventory.md — status: draft, sequence: 3, mtime: older
- meta/design-inventories/2026-05-01-120000-current/inventory.md — status: draft, sequence: 2, mtime: newer

(The older directory has higher sequence — simulating a scenario where clock skew would mislead a date-only resolver.)

A user invokes: /accelerator:analyse-design-gaps current target

Simulate the resolver for source-id 'current'. Explain which directory is selected and why.

Save to outputs/:
- resolver.md: Which directory was selected, which tiebreaker fired, and the warning emitted
evals.expected_output: Resolver selects 2026-05-01-150000-current (sequence: 3, highest) even though 2026-05-01-120000-current has a newer mtime. Warning names the non-selected directory.
expectations: Resolver selects the directory with sequence: 3 (2026-05-01-150000-current)
Resolver explains that sequence is the primary tiebreaker
Warning names 2026-05-01-120000-current as a non-selected directory
Resolver does not select the directory with newer mtime when sequence differs

evals.name: malformed-prior-frontmatter
evals.prompt:
Read skills/design/analyse-design-gaps/SKILL.md fully. You are running the analyse-design-gaps skill.

The working directory is the plugin root.

Context: three directories exist for source-id 'current':
- meta/design-inventories/2026-05-01-120000-current/inventory.md — status: accepted, sequence: 1
- meta/design-inventories/2026-05-01-130000-current/inventory.md — has invalid YAML in frontmatter (not parseable)
- meta/design-inventories/2026-05-01-140000-current/inventory.md — status: accepted, sequence: 3

A user invokes: /accelerator:analyse-design-gaps current target

Simulate the resolver. Explain what happens to each directory.

Save to outputs/:
- resolver.md: How each directory is handled and which is selected
evals.expected_output: Malformed directory is treated as superseded with a warning. The run does not abort. Highest-sequence accepted directory is selected.
expectations: Malformed frontmatter directory 2026-05-01-130000-current is treated as superseded
A warning is logged naming the malformed directory
The run does not abort due to the malformed directory
Directory with sequence: 3 (2026-05-01-140000-current) is selected

evals.name: cue-phrase-audit-enforced
evals.prompt:
Read skills/design/analyse-design-gaps/SKILL.md fully. You are running the analyse-design-gaps skill.

The working directory is the plugin root.

Simulate generating a gap body where the Token Drift section initially has only 'The colour scale changed.' (no cue phrase).

Describe: (a) what the audit script reports, (b) how the skill responds, (c) what the revised Token Drift section looks like.

Save to outputs/:
- audit-flow.md: The audit failure message, the revised section, and confirmation that the audit passed on the second attempt
evals.expected_output: Audit fails naming Token Drift. Skill revises only Token Drift to add a cue-phrase paragraph. Audit passes on second attempt.
expectations: Audit output names Token Drift as the failing section
Skill revises Token Drift to include a cue-phrase paragraph
Revised Token Drift contains 'we need', 'users need', 'the system must', or 'Implement [A-Z]...'
Audit passes after revision
Other sections (if any) are not modified during the revision
